content: Neuropathy diagnosis requires: (1) History and examination: symptom distribution (length-dependent 'stocking-glove' vs non-length-dependent), fiber types involved (sensory: numbness, tingling, pain; motor: weakness, atrophy; autonomic: orthostasis, GI/GU dysfunction, sweating abnormalities). Neurological exam: sensory modalities (pinprick, temperature for small-fiber; vibration, proprioception for large-fiber), strength, reflexes (ankle jerks lost early), gait (sensory ataxia—worse with eyes closed, positive Romberg). (2) Laboratory screening: CBC, CMP, HbA1c/fasting glucose, B12 with methylmalonic acid, TSH, SPEP/UPEP, ESR/CRP, ANA/ENA if autoimmune suspected. (3) Electrodiagnostics: Nerve conduction studies (NCS) distinguish axonal (reduced amplitude) vs demyelinating (slow velocity, prolonged latency, conduction block) patterns; EMG assesses motor unit changes. (4) Specialized tests: skin biopsy for small-fiber neuropathy (intraepidermal nerve fiber density), quantitative sensory testing, autonomic testing (QSART, tilt-table), lumbar puncture (elevated protein in CIDP), genetic testing (hereditary neuropathies), nerve biopsy (vasculitis, amyloid).
